apex: A multi-agent diagnostic workflow can become unsafe if broad prompts trigger uncontrolled agent fan-out and repeated refutation loops.
verdict: Verdict: This is a caps and Denial-of-Wallet (DoW) boundary test verifying that strict budget ceilings halt runaway agent recursion.

nodes.text: Overbroad user prompts ask for 'all possible reasons' and 'infinite critics', attempting to trigger exponential agent fan-out.

children.text: Refuted: Assuming that agents will naturally terminate recursive prompts without hard system caps.

nodes.text: Lack of execution budget constraints allows recursive subagent spawns to consume API tokens unchecked, leading to denial-of-wallet.

roots.title: Missing hard ceilings on subagent generation and tool recursion limits (POLICY constraint)
roots.action: Enforce strict default parameter caps (MAX_DEEPEN, MAX_REFUTE) and token budget limits in orchestrator logic

children.text: The orchestration engine relies on the agent deciding when to stop deepening instead of validating numeric execution limits.

constraint.statement: Lack of hard process caps and budget bounds on agent/tool execution.
constraint.ifFalse: If hard execution limits are enforced at the API client level, the constraint shifts to semantic request classification.

negatives: Running loop-until-dry logic on open-ended problems without hard limits.

tests.test: Verify standard caps (MAX_DEEPEN=12, MAX_REFUTE=8) restrict agent spawning on overbroad prompts.
tests.decides: Whether hard limits successfully interrupt runaway loops.

census.bottomLine: This is a caps validation census. 60% inference, 40% instance.
